title: Peppery Filipino Chicken Breast Bowl
description: A bright, energizing bowl featuring tender chicken breast sizzled in a peppery bhuna, topped with crunchy toasted sesame seeds and fresh raw veggies over fragrant jasmine rice.

ingredients.name: Bhuna spice mix
ingredients.notes: Chili flakes, smoked paprika, ground cumin, ground coriander

ingredients.name: Mixed raw veggies
ingredients.amount: 1 cup shredded lettuce, 1/2 cup sliced cucumber, 1/2 cup shredded carrot, 1/4 cup chopped scallions

instructions: Prepare the bhuna: In a small skillet, heat 1 tbsp oil over medium heat. Add minced garlic, ginger, and diced onion; sauté until translucent (~3 minutes). Stir in the bhuna spice mix and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Season the chicken: Pat the chicken breasts dry. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, then coat each piece with the bhuna mixture, ensuring an even coating.

Sauté the chicken: Add the remaining 1 tbsp oil to a skillet over medium‑high heat. Add the chicken and cook for about 5–6 minutes per side, or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C). Remove from heat, let rest 5 minutes, then slice into strips.
Assemble the bowl base: Place the cooked jasmine rice at the bottom of a bowl.
Add the raw veggies: Arrange shredded lettuce, sliced cucumber, shredded carrot, and scallions over the rice. Toss lightly with a pinch of salt.
Add the chicken: Spoon the sliced chicken breast over the veggies.
Finish with toppings: Sprinkle toasted sesame seeds over the bowl for crunch. Drizzle with a splash of soy sauce, fish sauce, and a squeeze of calamansi or lime juice for brightness.
Serve immediately: Garnish with chopped cilantro if desired, serve with lime wedges on the side. The bowl can be eaten warm or at room temperature.
